questions.title: Question Library
questions.description: Browse and practice JavaScript interview questions. Commit to an answer, then explore the explanation and run the code.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Type a keyword, concept, or question number...

question.fallbackNotice: This question is not yet translated into {locale} — showing in English.

landing.headline: Master JavaScript. One question at a time.
landing.subline: Execute code in-browser, visualize the event loop, and commit the mechanics to memory. A precision instrument for interview prep.

workflow.sectionSubline: A precision-engineered feedback cycle designed to commit advanced JavaScript to your long-term memory.
workflow.step1Desc: Filter by topic, dial in the difficulty, or search directly to a concept.
workflow.step2Desc: No peeking. Force yourself to commit before the explanation reveals.
workflow.step3Desc: Pop open the scratchpad. Execute snippets. See raw output immediately.
workflow.step4Desc: Deep dive into the explanation. Deconstruct the event loop and execution context.

dashboard.title: Practice Hub
dashboard.description: Track your JavaScript interview practice progress, review weak topics, and maintain your learning streak.
dashboard.statSummary: You've answered {count} questions across {topics} topics. Maintain your momentum.
dashboard.statHero: Start with one question, get immediate feedback, and build consistent practice habits.
dashboard.streakFire: You are on fire! Practice tomorrow to keep it going.
dashboard.noStreak: Practice today to start a new streak.
dashboard.activityDesc: Consistency is the key to mastery.
dashboard.needsPracticeDesc: Focus on these concepts to improve your mastery.
dashboard.reviewsDueDesc: You have {count} question(s) due for spaced repetition review. Master your concepts.

credits.label: Credits & Attribution
credits.description: Learn about the creators and contributors behind JS Questions Lab, including Lydia Hallie's original JavaScript questions.
credits.heroTitle: Built by {creator}, powered by Lydia Hallie's questions.
credits.heroBody: {name} is a creator-built practice product. The original questions and explanations come from Lydia Hallie's {source}. My contribution is the product layer: the interface, runner, visualization, scratchpad, and progress loop that make the material feel more usable in real practice sessions.
credits.heroNote: This page extends the original credits, it does not replace them. The upstream source references and supported language listing stay preserved below.
credits.whyTitle: A strong source deserved a stronger practice surface.
credits.whyBody1: Lydia's repository is one of the best JavaScript references on the internet, but interview prep usually needs a faster loop than reading a long markdown file. I built this so you can answer first, reveal second, run code immediately, and come back with progress that still means something.
credits.whyBody2: This project is also personal proof-of-work. I wanted the site itself to show care: design restraint, faster interactions, better mobile ergonomics, and a clearer sense that someone is actively making the experience better.
credits.addition1: A focused question library instead of a long README scroll
credits.addition2: Worker-based runnable snippets that stay inside the app
credits.addition4: Progress, review cues, bookmarks, and a cleaner practice loop
credits.originalBody1: The question content, explanations, and the original educational foundation all belong to that work and its contributors.
credits.originalBody2: The original repo links and the supported language files stay visible here so the source material remains easy to trace back to.
credits.languagesBody: These translation links come directly from Lydia Hallie's source material and stay part of the credits page so the original language coverage remains visible.
credits.originalFor: for creating and maintaining the source material this experience is built on.

meta.title: JS Questions Lab
meta.description: Interactive JavaScript interview practice built on Lydia Hallie's questions, with runnable snippets and event-loop visualizations.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Have questions about JS Questions Lab? Interested in sponsorships or collaborations? Reach out and get in touch.

contact.subheadline: Have questions about the platform? Interested in sponsorships or collaborations? Reach out and I'll get back to you.
contact.formSubtitle: Fill out the form below and I'll get back to you shortly.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.desc: Quick reference for the global, question list, and question detail keyboard controls.
shortcuts.toggleHint: Press <key>?</key> anytime to toggle this help dialog

progress.title: Your Progress
progress.subtitle: Track mastery by topic and continue where you left off.

ide.unlockRuntime: Submit an answer first to unlock the isolated worker runtime and event-loop tools.
ide.unlockDom: Submit an answer first to unlock the DOM event replay for this snippet.
ide.domHint: Use the replay panel below to inspect event.target, currentTarget, and bubbling order.
ide.staticNotice: This snippet is shown for context only. No interactive runtime is attached to this question.

scratchpad.title: Scratchpad
scratchpad.subtitle: Edit, run, and visualize isolated JavaScript code.

debugger.noDataDesc: Run your code to see a step-by-step visualization of the event loop, call stack, and task queues.
debugger.captionBegins: Main thread evaluates synchronous code, pushing frames onto the Call Stack.
debugger.captionEmpty: Synchronous execution completes. Event loop will now check for queued microtasks.
